text.title: Aesthetic and Minimalist Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Aesthetic and Minimalist Design. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Element prioritisation is driven by usage analytics, not designer taste; the top 80 percent of usage informs primary, the bottom 20 percent informs hide/remove.
rule.rationale: Without data, prioritisation reverts to opinion.

rule.statement: Rarely-needed information is hidden behind progressive disclosure (accordion, tab, expand) rather than removed outright when users still need it occasionally.
rule.rationale: Removing rarely-used items can break niche-but-critical flows.

rule.statement: Whitespace is enforced by tokens in the design system, not ad-hoc; reducing whitespace requires a token override with rationale.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc whitespace creates inconsistent breathing room across screens.

rule.statement: Each new feature added to an existing page passes a complexity-budget check; if the page exceeds the budget, an existing element must be moved or removed.
rule.rationale: Without a budget pages accumulate clutter indefinitely.

rule.statement: Removals are A/B tested or first-click tested before permanent removal; assumptions about what is rarely used can be wrong.
rule.rationale: Removing the wrong element breaks niche-but-critical workflows.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-aesthetic-minimalist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
